title: cerasus cyclamina var. biflora
intro: Cerasus cyclamina var. biflora, also known as the Chinese dogwood or multiflora rose, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its clusters of showy white flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and promoting digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
